$comment: Per-config request-validation settings for camunda-hub. enumCaseInsensitive: true — Hub accepts enum values case-insensitively, so a case-only mutation is NOT rejected with 400; the generator therefore omits case-only enum mutations rather than emitting them as false-positive 400-expecting tests. authAbsentMode: 'all-secured' — the Hub Public API v2 is uniformly Bearer-authenticated via a single global `security: [{ bearerAuth: [] }]` requirement and declares no `x-enforcement: conditional` schemes, so the 401 auth-absent surface is every secured op. authDenyMode: 'all-secured' — Hub checks body-validation (400) and resource-existence (404) before the authority gate (@PreAuthorize, 403), so only keyless, no-required-body, no-required-non-path-param secured ops are targeted (camunda/camunda-hub#25264). resourceFixtures (#352): map each resource-key name (path param OR body field) to the env var holding a REAL key the runner creates before tests run; wherever the generator would emit a filler ('x', or '1' from constraintViolations/parameters) for one of these, it emits `process.env[<ENV>] || '<filler>'` (|| so an unset OR empty env var falls back) so a malformed-field test rides on a valid envelope (the path resource exists / the referenced project|folder is accessible) and reaches body validation (400) instead of a 404 lookup or 403 access check on 'x'. parentFolderKey reuses the folder fixture. The body `projectKey` maps to RV_FIXTURE_V2_PROJECT_KEY (a ProcessApplication), matching pathResourceFixtures: createFile/createFolder/updateFile/updateFolder @PreAuthorize resolve the projectKey as a ProcessApplication, so a V2 key is the accessible move/create target that gets a malformed-field test past the 403 gate into body validation (a V1 `Project`/workspace id 403s). The runner creates the file/folder fixtures inside this V2 project too (scripts/e2e/run-hub.sh make_fixtures). knownIssue: excludeOperations entries may carry an optional knownIssue { summary, url, tracker? }, and a suite-wide knownIssues[] covers skips not tied to a single op; both feed the nightly's "skipped due to known issues" Slack thread. Entries sharing an issue url MUST share the same summary (deduped by url) — enforced by tests/codegen/known-issue-summary-consistency.test.ts.

excludeOperations.reason: Blocked on camunda/camunda-hub#25801 (tracked via #419): a version can't be created via the public API (every v2-creatable file lives inside a process application, which can't be versioned), so RV_FIXTURE_VERSION_KEY is empty and updateVersion's negative tests 404 on the missing version instead of reaching body validation (400). Excluded from the negative suite until versioning is unblocked.

knownIssue.summary: updateVersion / restoreVersion — versioning blocked, no version fixture can exist

excludeOperations.reason: Blocked on camunda/camunda-hub#25801 (tracked via #419): same as updateVersion — no version can exist, so restoreVersion's negative tests 404 instead of 400. Excluded until versioning is unblocked.

knownIssue.summary: updateVersion / restoreVersion — versioning blocked, no version fixture can exist

excludeOperations.reason: Its missing-required-multipart-part cases hit camunda-hub#26448 (ProblemDetail missing `type` on Spring's own framework-level validation errors). Its 2 auth-absent/auth-invalid cases are separately the camunda-hub#26447 pattern (see knownProblemDetailShapeGaps below) — those two ARE otherwise-valid, still-passing status-code coverage; excluding the whole op drops them too rather than building a third (operationId + scenarioKind) suppression mechanism just to save 2 tests per op across the 2 ops affected here. Accepted as a small, disclosed cost, not a coverage-neutral exclusion.

knownIssue.summary: ProblemDetail missing `type` on Spring's own framework-level validation errors (missing/malformed multipart parts, missing/malformed query params)

excludeOperations.reason: Its missing/wrong-type-on-required-query-param cases (id/start/end) hit camunda-hub#26448: Spring's own MissingServletRequestParameterException/MethodArgumentTypeMismatchException handling returns a ProblemDetail missing `type` (confirmed live — same root cause as the multipart case, broader than the issue's original title). Its 2 auth-absent/auth-invalid cases are separately the camunda-hub#26447 pattern (see knownProblemDetailShapeGaps below) and are otherwise-valid, still-passing status-code coverage; excluding the whole op drops those 2 too, same disclosed tradeoff as ingestCatalogAssets above.

knownIssue.summary: ProblemDetail missing `type` on Spring's own framework-level validation errors (missing/malformed multipart parts, missing/malformed query params)

knownIssues.summary: Wrong-type tests on resource-key body fields (projectKey, folderKey, …) are skipped — Hub 403s/500s on a malformed key instead of 400

knownProblemDetailShapeGaps.reason: Live-verified (hub-ondemand-test.yml run 29422042438): every 401 response returns a completely empty body instead of a ProblemDetail — Spring Security's authentication entry point rejects the request before the app's own ProblemDetail assembly ever runs. Systemic across ~39 operations, so excluding each op (excludeOperations) would drop its other, unaffected negative coverage too. The status-code assertion for these scenarios is unaffected — only the shape check is skipped.

knownIssue.summary: Every 401 response returns an empty body instead of a ProblemDetail
